---
title: "Research Objective: Sales Channel and Spending Patterns"
author: "Michelle Diaz"
format: html
---

## Research Objective

This section investigates whether a customer's sales channel (online vs. in-store) relates to their spending patterns. As part of our group's customer segmentation project using H&M transaction data, this RO aims to segment customers by channel preference and compare spending behavior across those segments.

## Planned IV's and DV's

- Independent variable: sales_channel_id (online vs. store purchase)
- Dependent variable: price (per transaction) and/or total spend per customer

## Data Wrangling Plan

- Join transaction-level data (sales_channel_id, price) with customer and article data as needed
- Check sales_channel_id for correct coding and confirm both channel categories are represented
- Aggregate price to customer-level total spend, if analyzing at the customer rather than transaction level
- Check for and address any missing or invalid price values

## Data Visualization Plan

- Boxplot or violin plot comparing price distribution across the two sales channels
- Bar chart comparing average spend per customer by channel
- Consider a density plot to show spending distribution shape by channel

## Notes

This RO uses a sampled subset of the H&M transactions_train.csv data (due to the full file's size), joined with customer and article metadata as needed.


## Github Repo 
https://github.com/MichelleeeDiazzz/IBM-6540-Group-Project-
